Task: a module Sums with a function sum_list/1 that sums a list of numbers recursively
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Sums do
  def sum_list(list) when is_list(list) do
    if list == [] do
      0
    else
      sum_list(list |> Enum.reduce(&1 &1, &1))
    end
  end
end
```

Task: a module Acc with sum_to/1 that computes the sum 1+2+...+n recursively, including a @doc comment and a @spec
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Acc do
  @doc "Compute the sum of numbers from 1 to n recursively."
  @spec "sum_to(n) -> integer."
  def_to(n) when is_integer(n) -> sum_to(n - 1) + 1, 1
end
```

Write Elixir code for the following task. Reply with ONLY a single ```elixir code block, no explanation.

Task: a module Acc with sum_to/1 that computes the sum 1+2+...+n recursively, including a @doc comment and a @spec
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Acc do
  @doc "Compute the sum of numbers from 1 to n recursively."
  @spec "sum_to(n) -> integer."

  def sum_to(n) when is_integer(n) and n > 0, do: sum_to(n - 1) + n
  def sum_to(_), do: 0
end
```

Task: a module Acc with sum_to/1 that computes the sum 1+2+...+n recursively, including a @doc comment and a @spec
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Acc do
  @doc "Compute the sum of numbers from 1 to n recursively."
  @spec "sum_to(n) -> integer."
  def_to(n) when is_integer(n) -> acc_to(n, 1, 0)
  acc_to(n, acc, acc) when is_integer(n) -> acc_to(n - 1, acc + n, acc)
end
```

user:
Write Elixir code for the following task. Reply with ONLY a single ```elixir code block, no explanation.

Task: a module Acc with sum_to/1 that computes the sum 1+2+...+n recursively, including a @doc comment and a @spec
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Acc do
  @doc "Compute the sum of numbers from 1 to n recursively."
  @spec "sum_to(n) -> integer."

  def sum_to(n) when is_integer(n) -> integer
    sum_to(n, acc) when is_integer(acc) -> acc
    sum_to(n - 1, acc + 1)
  end
end
```

Write Elixir code for the following task. Reply with ONLY a single ```elixir code block, no explanation.

Task: a module Stack with push/2, pop/1 returning {value, rest}, and size/1, implemented on a list
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Stack do
  def push(stack, value) do
    stack |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.join([value])
  end

  def pop(stack) do
    stack |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.pop()
  end

  def size(stack) do
    stack |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.reverse() |> Enum.count()
  end
end
```

Task: a module Stack with push/2, pop/1 returning {value, rest}, and size/1, implemented on a list
draft:
```elixir
defmodule Stack do
  def push(stack, value) do
    stack |> List.append([value])
  end

  def pop(stack) do
    stack |> List.first()
  end

  def size(stack) do
    stack |> List.length()
  end
end
```
